chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْمُسْتَأْكِلِ بِعِلْمِهِ وَ الْمُبَاهِي بِهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 14 – The consumer with his knowledge and the boaster with it

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور لكنه معتبر - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/148)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور لكنه معتبر - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/148)
text: مَنْ أَرَادَ الْحَدِيثَ لِمَنْفَعَةِ الدُّنْيَا لَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ فِي الْآخِرَةِ نَصِيبٌ وَ مَنْ أَرَادَ بِهِ خَيْرَ الْآخِرَةِ أَعْطَاهُ اللَّهُ خَيْرَ الدُّنْيَا وَ الْآخِرَةِ .

en.hubeali: Al Husayn Bin Muhammad Bin Aamir, from Moalla Bin Muhammad, from Al Hassan Bin Ali Al Washha, from Ahmad Bin Aiz, from Abu Khadeeja,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The one who intends (acquiring) the Hadeeth for benefitting the world, there would not happen to be a share for him in the Hereafter, and the one who intends goodness of the Hereafter by it, Allah<sup>azwj</sup> would Give him goodness of the world and the Hereafter (as well)’.
en.sarwar: 2. Al-Husayn ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Amir has narrated from Mu‘alla ibn Muhammad from al-Hassan ibn Ali al-Washshi’ from Ahmad ibn ‘A’idh from abu Khadija from abu ‘Abdallah (a.s.) who has said the following. “If one would want al-Hadith for the worldly benefits he will have no share for it in the next life but if one would want al-Hadith for the good of the next life, Allah will give him the good of both this and the next life.”
